title: ARGENTUM Reputation Dispute — False Action Claim
description: A poster is accused of fabricating or exaggerating a completed action recorded in the ARGENTUM karma economy.
question: Should the disputed action be slashed (ruling 1) or cleared (ruling 2)?

descriptions: The dispute is invalid, out of scope, or lacks sufficient information. No karma is slashed.
The poster loses karma equal to the action's karma value. Attestors who signed off may be slashed in a follow-up dispute.
The action is valid as recorded. The reporter loses their bond.

evidence_expected: The action's own proof link or artifact
Counter-evidence that the claim is false or exaggerated (commits, logs, third-party references)
Context including timestamps and independent witnesses
